codeReview.summary: Pass. I reviewed the original failed feature/review, the fix handoff, the fix transcript skeleton, commit `1ec2e73`, and the current source. The blocking taxonomy mismatch is resolved: `src/content/use-case-analyses.ts:286-337` now keeps whale-exclusive analysis focused on whale-premium products and moves enterprise data-licensing analysis into its own `util-enterprise-data-licensing` entry, while `src/components/tokenomics/TokenUtilitySurface.tsx:184-205` renders `Whale Exclusive Products` and `Enterprise Data Licensing` as distinct cards. The new regression coverage in `src/components/tokenomics/__tests__/use-case-taxonomy-alignment.test.tsx:23-169` specifically asserts that whale-exclusives no longer renders enterprise data-licensing copy and that the enterprise card is present separately. I also verified the full mapping in the shipped source: the surface now exposes 11 utility-card IDs and `USE_CASE_ANALYSES` provides matching analysis entries for all 11, with no missing or extra use-case IDs.

summary: Pass. The rerun fully fixes the prior blocking issue: enterprise/API data licensing now appears on its own card, the Whale Exclusive Products card shows only whale-premium analysis, and the current utility-card/analysis IDs are aligned one-to-one. Review report written to the scrutiny reviews directory.
